armored_battlemage.name: Armored Battlemage
description: ## Armored Battlemage
‹SOURCE Armor Master's Handbook/4›

Though it hasn't been at war with its southern neighbor for centuries, Nex continues to train battlemages at its arcane schools in Ecanus. Many battlemages focus purely on destructive evocations, but some extend their tactical studies to include use of the tried-and-true protection of steel. These armored battlemages learn to move and cast spells in even the most restrictive armors, and have developed new methods to magically enhance their armor. Most armored battlemages strongly support the Arclords of Nex. Many travel beyond their homelands seeking some method of contacting the archmage Nex, and hope for some excuse to convince the Council of Three and Nine to renew hostilities with the nation of Geb.

::ab[Medium Armor (Ex)]{icon=power-def ability="An armored battlemage gains this ability at 1st level instead of 7th. When wearing medium or heavy armor, the armored battlemage gains a +4 bonus on ‹rule/concentration› checks to cast defensively. This stacks with the bonus from the ‹feat/Combat Casting› feat." replace="Spell combat" alter="Medium armor"}

::ab[Arcane Pool (Su)]{icon=down-def ability="An armored battlemage cannot spend points from his *arcane pool* to enhance weapons. Instead, he can expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* as a @HLswift_action to grant armor he is wearing a +1 enhancement bonus for 1 minute." increment="The enhancement bonus~1~4" imp5="These bonuses can be used to add any of the following armor special abilities: ‹magic-enh/balanced›, ‹magic-enh/bitter›, ‹magic-enh/fortification «(heavy, »light«, or medium)›, ‹magic-enh/ghost touch<_a›, ‹magic-enh/invulnerability›, ‹magic-enh/spell resistance (13«, 15, 17, or 19)›, or ‹magic-enh/spell storing<_a›. Adding these special abilities consumes an amount of bonus equal to the ability's base price modifier. In addition, the armored battlemage can grant his armor the ‹magic-enh/energy resistance› special ability at the cost of a +3 bonus or the ‹magic-enh/improved energy resistance› special ability at the cost of a +5 bonus. These special abilities are added to any the armor already has, but duplicates do not stack." special="These bonuses stack with existing armor enhancement bonuses to a maximum of +5. If the armor is not magical, at least a +1 enhancement bonus must be added before any other special abilities can be added. These bonuses and special abilities are decided when the *arcane pool* point is spent and cannot be changed until the next time the armored battlemage uses this ability. These bonuses do not function if the armor is worn by anyone other than the magus. Multiple uses of this ability do not stack with themselves. An armored battlemage can enhance only one suit of armor in this way at one time. If he uses this ability again, the first use immediately ends." alter="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{icon=power head l3="An armored battlemage gains *armor training,* as per the ‹class/fighter› ability, although some of its benefits are delayed (see below)." replace="Magus arcana gained at 3rd and 18th levels~improved spell combat"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{icon=boost next flavor="Text from the fighter class ability, with levels adjusted." l3="Whenever you wear armor, you reduce the armor check penalty by 1 (to a minimum of 0) and increase the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by the armor by 1. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing medium armor." l8="The penalty is now reduced by 2 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +2. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing heavy armor." l13="The penalty is now reduced by 3 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +3." l18="The penalty is now reduced by 4 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +4."}
::ab[Heavy Armor (Ex)]{icon=power ability="An armored battlemage gains this ability at 7th level instead of 13th." alter="Heavy armor"}

::ab[Expanded Arcane Pool (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost ability="The armored battlemage can grant his armor the ‹magic-enh/glamered› special ability at the cost of a +2 bonus, the ‹magic-enh/slick› or ‹magic-enh/shadow› special ability at the cost of a +3 bonus, and the ‹magic-enh/greater slick› or ‹magic-enh/greater shadow› special ability at the cost of a +5 bonus when using his *arcane pool.*" replace="Greater spell combat"}

beastblade.name: Beastblade
description: ## Beastblade
‹SOURCE Familiar Folio/8›
Beastblade magi work in tandem with their familiars, using spell, steel, and claw to clear the battlefield of foes.
::ab[Familiar (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="The beastblade gains the ‹arcana/familiar› magus arcana." replace="Magus arcana gained at 3rd level"}

::ab[Tandem Touch (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="While the beastblade's familiar is holding the charge for a touch spell, the beastblade can cast another spell without discharging the familiar's held charge." replace="Spell recall"}

::ab[Familiar Pool (Su)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="When a beastblade prepares his magus spells, he can expend points from his *arcane pool* (up to a maximum number of points equal to his Intelligence modifier) to empower his familiar. For each point expended in this way, the familiar can cast one magus spell as a spell-like ability once that day. Each spell selected must be 3 spell levels lower than the highest spell level the beastblade can cast, and can't require a material component that costs more than 1 gp." replace="Knowledge pool"}

::ab[Familiar Spellstrike (Su)]{l=11 icon=lower passive="Whenever a beastblade's familiar successfully delivers a touch spell against a creature the magus threatens, the target provokes an attack of opportunity from the magus." replace="Improved spell recall"}

bladebound.name: Bladebound
description: ## Bladebound
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/47›

A select group of magi are called to carry a @HL[black blade]: a sentient weapon of often unknown and possibly unknowable purpose. These weapons become valuable tools and allies, as both the magus and weapon typically crave arcane power, but as a *black blade* becomes more aware, its true motivations manifest, and as does its ability to influence its wielder with its ever-increasing ego.
### Black Blades

A *black blade* is a particular form of ‹rule/intelligent «weapon<items› gained by a magus with the bladebound archetype. There are several ways a magus might gain this weapon. Sometimes it just appears among the magus's possessions, and its origin is a mystery. Other times the magus finds a *black blade* during an adventure or event of some kind. Sometimes a *black blade* is passed down generation to generation in an ongoing search for a magus who can unlock its true potential. A *black blade* is always a one-handed slashing weapon, a ‹eq-weapon/rapier›, or a ‹eq-weapon/sword cane›. The magus chooses the blade's type upon gaining the blade, and once chosen, it can't be changed. As a bladebound magus increases in level, his *black blade* gains power. A *black blade* is independently conscious but features some personality traits reflecting its wielder. A *black blade* always has the same alignment as its wielder and even changes its alignment if its wielder does. The blade typically works toward its wielder's goals, but not always without argument or backlash. Each *black blade* has a mission, and while sometimes two or more *black blades* will work in concert, each mission is singular in purpose (the *black blade's* mission is usually up to the GM and the needs of the campaign or the adventure, or a GM can roll randomly for the weapon's purpose using the ‹rule/special>Intelligent Item» Purpose<_items› table). Some *black blades* are very open about their missions, but most are secretive. Certain sages have speculated that an invisible hand or arcane purpose moves these weapons.
| Magus Class Level | Enhancement Bonus | Int | Wis/Cha | Ego | Special |
| 3rd-4th | +1 | 11 | 7 | 5 | Alertness, black blade strike, telepathy, unbreakable |
| 5th-6th | +2 | 12 | 8 | 8 | Energy attunement |
| 9th-10th | +3 | 14 | 10 | 12 | Teleport blade |
| 13th-14th | +4 | 16 | 12 | 16 | Transfer arcana |
| 17th-18th | +5 | 18 | 14 | 22 | Spell defense |
| 19th-20th | +5 | 19 | 15 | 24 | Life drinker |

::ab[Black Blade (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-melee-down ability="The bladebound magus' gains a powerful sentient weapon called a @HLblack_blade, whose weapon type is chosen by the magus. A magus with this class feature cannot take the ‹arcana/familiar› magus arcana, and cannot have a familiar of any kind, even from another class." special="Instead of the normal *arcane pool* amount, the bladebound magus's *arcane pool* has a number of points equal to ‹misc/one-third› of his magus level (minimum 1) plus his Intelligence bonus." alter="Arcane Pool" replace="Magus arcana gained at 3rd level"}
:::ab{title="Black Blade Basics" icon=info action=Info}
A *black blade* is bonded to a particular magus, much like a familiar, but in more of a partnership than a master-servant relationship.
>@HL[Intelligence:] This is the intelligence score of the *black blade.* It starts at 10 and increases by 1 for every two levels of the bladebound magus (at 3rd level, 5th level, and so on).
>@HL[Wisdom and Charisma:] As the bladebound magus increases in level, so do the Wisdom and Charisma of the *black blade.* These abilities start at 6 and increase by 1 for every two levels of magus.

>@HL[Ego:] A *black blade* starts with an ego of 5, and that ego increases as the blade becomes more powerful. In cases where a wielder and the *black blade* come into conflict, like any intelligent item, a *black blade* can attempt to exert its dominance using the ‹rule/items against characters› rules. Due to its flexible and powerful nature, a *black blade* has a non-standard ego progression.

>@HL[Languages and Skills:] A *black blade* starts with Common as a language. As the *black blade* increases in Intelligence, it manifests knowledge of languages and arcane lore. Upon reaching an Intelligence of 12, it gains a bonus language of the GM's choice, and gains 1 rank in Knowledge (arcana). Each time the sword gains a bonus to Intelligence, it gains another language and another rank in Knowledge (arcana).

>@HL[Senses:] A *black blade* is aware of everything around it like a creature that can see and hear. It can be blinded and deafened as if it were a creature. It uses the saving throws of its magus, even if the magus is not currently wielding the *black blade.*
>@HL[Black Blade Arcane Pool:] A *black blade* has an *arcane pool* with a number of points equal to 1 + its Intelligence bonus.

::ab[Black Blade Ability Descriptions]{head icon=power ability="A *black blade* has special abilities (or imparts abilities to its wielder) depending on the wielder's magus level. The abilities are cumulative. A *black blade* normally refuses to use any of its abilities when wielded by anyone other than its magus, and acts as a ‹rule/masterwork w«eapon› of its type."}
::ab[Alertness (Ex)]{next icon=power ability="While a magus is wielding his *black blade,* he gains the ‹feat/Alertness› feat."}

::ab[Black Blade Strike (Sp)]{next icon=boost free="The magus can spend a point from the *black blade's arcane pool* to grant the *black blade* a +1 bonus on damage rolls for 1 minute." increment="The bonus~1~4"}

::ab[Telepathy (Su)]{next icon=power ability="While a magus is wielding or carrying his *black blade,* he can communicate telepathically with the blade in a language that the magus and the *black blade* share."}

::ab[Unbreakable (Ex)]{next icon=def ability="As long as it has at least 1 point in its *arcane pool,* a *black blade* is immune to the ‹misc/broken› condition. If broken, the *black blade* is unconscious and powerless until repaired." special="If destroyed, the *black blade* can be reforged 1 week later through a special ritual that costs 200 gp per magus level. The ritual takes 24 hours to complete."}

::ab[Energy Attunement (Su)]{next l=5 icon=boost free="A magus can spend a point of his *black blade's arcane pool* to have it deal one of the following types of damage instead of weapon damage: cold, electricity, or fire. He can spend 2 points from the *black blade's arcane pool* to deal sonic or force damage instead of weapon damage. This effect lasts until the start of the magus's next turn."}

::ab[Teleport Blade (Sp)]{next icon=magic l=9 standard="A magus can expend an arcane point from his or his *black blade's arcane pool,* and can call his *black blade* from as far as 1 mile away, causing it to instantaneously teleport to his hand."}

::ab[Transfer Arcana (Su)]{next l=13 icon=magic fullround="Once per day, a magus can attempt to siphon points from his *black blade's arcane pool* into his own *arcane pool.* The magus must succeed at a Will saving throw with a DC equal to the *black blade's* ego. If the magus succeeds, he regains 1 point to his *arcane pool* for every 2 points he saps from his *black blade.* If he fails the saving throw, the magus becomes ‹misc/fatigued› (but can try again). If he is fatigued, he becomes ‹misc/exhausted› instead. He cannot use this ability if he is exhausted."}

::ab[Spell Defense (Sp)]{next l=17 icon=def free="A magus can expend an arcane point from his weapon's *arcane pool;* he then gains SR equal to his *black blade's* ego until the start of his next turn."}
:::ab{next title="Life Drinker (Su)" l=19 icon=power-aid action=Ability special="The creature killed must have a number of Hit Dice equal to half the magus's character level for this to occur."}
Each time the magus kills a living creature with the *black blade,* he can pick one of the following effects:
- The *black blade* restores 2 points to its *arcane pool.*
- The *black blade* restores 1 point to its *arcane pool* and the magus restores 1 point to his *arcane pool.*
- The magus gains a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to the *black blade's* ego (these temporary hit points last until spent or 1 minute, whichever is shorter).
@HL[Magus Arcana:] The following magus arcana complement the bladebound magus archetype: ‹arcana/arcane accuracy›, ‹arcana/broad study›, ‹arcana/dispelling strike›, and ‹arcana/reflection›.

card_caster.name: Card Caster
description: ## Card Caster
‹SOURCE The Harrow Handbook/24›

Though it is known primarily as a tool of knowledge, the harrow is also an instrument of defense and even war. The card caster is an ancient, martial offshoot of the traditional harrower, learning to not only draw power from the harrow, but also to invest each card with deadly power.

::ab[Deadly Dealer (Su)]{l=1 icon=power ability="The card caster gains ‹feat/Deadly Dealer› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. A card caster can invest cards with enough magic to deal lethal damage even without the ‹feat/Arcane Strike› feat so long as he has at least 1 point in his *arcane pool.*"}

::ab[Arcane Pool Focus (Su)]{icon=down ability="A card caster's *arcane pool* can be used to augment only ranged weapons." imp5="A card caster can use his *arcane pool* to add the following weapon special abilities to ranged weapons: ‹magic-enh/brilliant energy›, ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/returning›, ‹magic-enh/seeking›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, and ‹magic-enh/speed›. He can use his *arcane pool* to augment an entire ‹eq-misc/harrow deck› as if it were ammunition. All cards from a deck enhanced with a special ability, such as flaming, must share the same bonus." alter="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Harrowed Spellstrike (Su)]{l=2 icon=ranged-magic ability="A card caster can invest a single thrown weapon with a single touch or ranged spell as part of the spell's normal casting time. The spell must target a single creature, and the spell's range changes to match the thrown weapon's range increment. This ability otherwise functions identically to *spellstrike,* except it can only be applied to thrown ranged weapons instead of melee attacks." replace=Spellstrike}

::ab[Role Dealer (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="Whenever the card caster randomly draws and throws a harrow card matching one axis of his alignment, the card's critical threat range increases to 19-20. If the card caster draws and throws a harrow card matching his alignment exactly, the attack's critical multiplier also increases to &times&3 and the magus gains a +4 bonus on his confirmation roll." replace="Magus arcana gained at 3rd level"}

deep_marshal.name: Deep Marshal
description: ## Deep Marshal
‹SOURCE Arcane Anthology/28›

According to dwarven legend, the first deep marshals were handpicked by King Taargick himself to create massive tunnels to aid in the dwarven exodus to Golarion's surface during the Quest for Sky. Largely unseen by visitors, the deep marshals have survived into the modern era as keepers, protectors, and repairers of the myriad of stony passages that travel around, through, and under the Five Kings Mountains. Although the vast majority of deep marshals are ‹race/dwarves›, a few non-dwarves have earned enough trust to be trained as deep marshals, or have learned the same techniques from studying old dwarven spellbooks.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=boost ability="A deep marshal is proficient with light armor, medium armor, and shields (except tower shields). A deep marshal can cast magus spells while wearing light armor or medium armor without incurring the normal spell failure chance." imp9="A deep marshal gains the heavy armor magus ability at 9th level." alter="Armor proficiencies~medium armor~heavy armor"}

::ab[Deep Spellcasting]{icon=learn-down order=an ability="A deep marshal learns numerous arcane secrets involving the shaping and warding of earth and stone. She adds the following ‹main/spells>sorcerer»/wizard« spells› to her magus spell list as magus spells of the same level: ‹spell/crafter's curse›, ‹spell/crafter's fortune›, ‹spell/erase›, ‹spell/expeditious excavation›, ‹spell/fabricate›, ‹spell/greater make whole›, ‹spell/knock›, ‹spell/major creation›, ‹spell/make whole›, ‹spell/mending›, ‹spell/minor creation›, ‹spell/move earth›, ‹spell/passwall›, ‹spell/stone shape›, ‹spell/transmute mud to rock›, ‹spell/transmute rock to mud›, and all 0- through 6th-level sorcerer/wizard spells of the ‹main/spells_>abjuration« school›. She must still add such spells to her spellbook to prepare them." info="If a spell appears on both the magus and sorcerer/wizard spell lists, the magus uses the lower of the two spell levels listed for the spell." special="A deep marshal cannot cast spells from the enchantment, illusion, or necromancy school and she cannot activate spell completion or spell trigger magic items that use spells from those schools (without making a successful ‹skill/Use Magic Device› check)." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Bound by Tradition]{icon=down info="A deep marshal is limited in how she can use her arcane powers. Her *arcane pool* has a number of points equal to 1/3 her magus level (minimum 1) + her Intelligence modifier. Furthermore, she cannot use her *arcane pool* to grant an enhancement bonus or weapon special ability to a weapon she is holding unless it is a ‹eq-weapon/battleaxe›, ‹eq-weapon/heavy pick›, ‹eq-weapon/warhammer›, or weapon with the word &quot&dwarven&quot& in its name. She can use her *spell combat* or *spellstrike* abilities only with such weapons." alter="Arcane pool~spell combat~spellstrike"}

::ab[Miner's Focus (Su/Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A deep marshal can use the magical properties of her weapon in order to augment her spellcasting. Whenever she casts a magus spell while wielding a magic battleaxe, heavy pick, warhammer, or weapon with the word &quot&dwarven&quot& in its name, she adds half the weapon's enhancement bonus to the her caster level (minimum +1). When attempting a caster level check to overcome spell resistance, she instead adds her weapon's full enhancement bonus." order=ap passive="In addition, she is treated as having a number of ranks in Knowledge (dungeoneering) and Profession (miner) equal to her magus level, and she attempts Profession (miner) checks using her Intelligence modifier instead of her Wisdom modifier." replace="Magus arcana gained at 3rd level"}

eldritch_archer.name: Eldritch Archer
description: ## Eldritch Archer
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/11›
The eldritch archer rains magical attacks down on her foes from the city walls.
::cskill{gain=per lose=umd}

::ab[Arcane Pool (Su)]{icon=power-down l=5 info="An eldritch archer's choices of weapon special abilities to apply with *arcane pool* include ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/nimble shot›, and ‹magic-enh/returning› but not dancing, keen, or vorpal." alter="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Ranged Weapon Bond (Ex)]{head l=1 icon=power ability="An eldritch archer gains a *bonded object* as per the ‹class/wizard«'s› *arcane bonded object;* it must be a ranged weapon, and it can't be used to cast a spell once per day. Holding her bonded item does not prevent the eldritch archer from providing somatic components for her spells."}
:::ab{next flavor="Text from the wizard class" title="Bonded Object" icon=magic action=Ability}

If you select a bonded object, you begin play with one at no cost. These objects are always masterwork quality. Weapons acquired at 1st level are not made of any special material. If you attempt to cast a spell without your bonded object wielded, you must make a ‹rule/concentration› check or lose the spell. The DC for this check is equal to 20 + the spell's level.

You can add additional magic abilities to your bonded object as if you have the required item creation feats and if you meet the level prerequisites of the feat. For example, a wizard with a bonded dagger must be at least 5th level to add magic abilities to the dagger (see ‹feat/Craft Magic Arms and Armor›). The magic properties of a bonded object, including any magic abilities added to the object, only function for the wizard who owns it. If a bonded object's owner dies, or the item is replaced, the object reverts to being an ordinary masterwork item of the appropriate type.

If a bonded object is damaged, it is restored to full hit points the next time you prepare your spells. If the object of an arcane bond is lost or destroyed, it can be replaced after 1 week in a special ritual that costs 200 gp per wizard level plus the cost of the masterwork item. This ritual takes 8 hours to complete. Items replaced in this way do not possess any of the additional enchantments of the previous bonded item. You can designate an existing magic item as his bonded item. This functions in the same way as replacing a lost or destroyed item except that the new magic item retains its abilities while gaining the benefits and drawbacks of becoming a bonded item.

::ab[Ranged Spell Combat (Ex)]{icon=power-ranged ability="Instead of a light or one-handed melee weapon, an eldritch archer must use a ranged weapon for *spell combat.* She doesn't need a free hand for ranged *spell combat.* The eldritch archer cannot accept an attack penalty to gain a bonus on ‹rule/concentration› checks to cast a spell defensively." alter="Spell combat"}

::ab[Ranged Spellstrike (Su)]{l=2 icon=ranged-zap ability="Whenever an eldritch archer casts a spell that calls for a ranged attack, she can deliver the spell through a ranged weapon she wields as part of a ranged attack. Instead of the free ranged attack normally allowed to deliver the spell, an eldritch archer can make one free ranged attack with a ranged weapon (at her highest base attack bonus) as part of casting this spell. The attack does not increase the spell's range." special="If the spell can normally affect multiple targets, only a single missile, ray, or effect accompanies each attack; if the spell allows multiple attacks and the eldritch archer can make additional ranged attacks as part of a full-round action with *spell combat,* one additional ray, missile, or effect from the spell accompanies each subsequent ranged attack the eldritch archer makes in the same round until all attacks allowed by the spell are made. Unused missiles, rays, or effects remaining at the end of the eldritch archer's turn are wasted." alter=Spellstrike}

::ab[Focusing Spellstrike (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic ability="An eldritch archer can use *ranged spellstrike* to deliver a cone- or line-shaped spell as a ray, affecting only the creature or object struck." replace=Counterstrike}

eldritch_scion.name: Eldritch Scion
description: ## Eldritch Scion
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/104›

Unlike typical magi, eldritch scions do not study tomes of magic or spend time learning to combine martial and magical skills. Rather, eldritch scions find that their spells and abilities come to them instinctively.
:::ab{title=Spells action=Ability icon=magic replace=Spells}

An eldritch scion casts arcane spells drawn from the magus spell list. He can cast any spell he knows without preparing it ahead of time. To learn or cast a spell, an eldritch scion must have a Charisma score equal to at least 10 + the spell's level. The DC for a saving throw against an eldritch scion's spell is 10 + the spell's level + the eldritch scion's Charisma modifier. An eldritch scion can cast only a certain number of spells of each spell level per day. His base daily spell allotment is the same as a bard of the same level. In addition, he receives bonus spells per day if he has a high Charisma score.

An eldritch scion's selection of spells is limited. He has the same number of spells known as a bard of the same level, and can choose new spells to replace old ones at 5th level and every 3 class levels after that, just as a bard does. See the ‹class/bard› for more information on swapping spells known.

::ab[Bloodline]{icon=power ability="An eldritch scion gains a ‹ability/bloodrager bloodline<s›. The bloodline is selected at 1st level, and this choice cannot be changed. An eldritch scion's effective bloodrager level for his bloodline abilities is equal to his eldritch scion level. He does not gain any bonus feats, and he gains bonus spells from his bloodline at different levels (see the *bonus spells* class feature below). To use any ability that normally functions when in a bloodrage, an eldritch scion must spend a point from his *eldritch pool* (see below)." special="If an eldritch scion takes levels in another class that grant a bloodline, the bloodlines must be the same type, even if that means that the bloodline of one of the classes must change. Subject to GM discretion, an eldritch scion can change his former bloodline to make them conform." replace="Spell recall"}

:::ab{title="Eldritch Pool (Su)" icon=magic action=Ability replace="Arcane pool" imp4="An eldritch scion can also use his *eldritch pool* as an *arcane pool,* gaining all the benefits listed with the magus's *arcane pool* class feature." special="Abilities that modify *arcane pool* also modify *eldritch pool.*"}

An eldritch scion gains an *eldritch pool* of personal magical energy, equal to 1/2 his magus level (minimum 1) + his Charisma modifier. As a @HL[swift action], he can spend a point of eldritch energy to enter a state of mystical focus for 2 rounds. This allows him to use abilities from his bloodrager bloodline as though he were in a bloodrage, though he gains none of the other benefits or drawbacks of bloodraging.

Additionally, any magus's class feature or spell from the magus spell list that normally uses a calculation based on Intelligence is instead based on Charisma for an eldritch scion. For example, an eldritch scion with the ‹arcana/arcane accuracy› magus arcana grants himself an insight bonus on attacks equal to his Charisma bonus, not his Intelligence bonus. This has no effect on the eldritch scion's skills or skill points.

::ab[Spell Combat (Ex)]{icon=down ability="An eldritch scion can only use *spell combat* while in a state of mystic focus (see *eldritch pool,* above)." imp8="An eldritch scion can use *spell combat* at any time." alter="Spell combat"}

::ab[Bonus Spells]{l=7 icon=learn ability="An eldritch scion gains the bonus spell from his bloodrager bloodline that is normally gained at 10th level." incrementOrd="He gains~7~9" incrementMax=14 incrementDesc=the incrementEnd=" bonus spell from his bloodline." replace="Knowledge pool"}
::ab[Improved Spell Combat (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power ability="An eldritch scion gains the *improved spell combat* class feature." alter="Improved spell combat"}
::ab[Greater Spell Combat (Ex)]{l=18 icon=power ability="An eldritch scion gains the *greater spell combat* class feature." alter="Greater spell combat"}

elemental_knight.name: Elemental Knight
description: ## Elemental Knight
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/203›

Elemental knights are born with elemental energies surging through their blood and discover the secret of reconciling and focusing this primal power into the arcane. An elemental knight has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Suli}
::ab[Elemental Arcana (Su)]{head l=3 icon=power ability="Elemental knights may select the following magus arcana available only to them."}

::ab[Assault Synergy (Su)]{next icon=boost swift="The elemental knight can expend 1 round of duration from *elemental assault* to add +1d6 points of energy damage to an offensive spell he casts that round. The spell must have the acid, cold, electricity or fire descriptor. This extra energy damage matches the energy damage of the spell." imp10="He may expend 2 rounds instead of 1 to add +2d6 points of energy damage to the spell."}

::ab[Energy Reflection (Su)]{next icon=def prereq="12th-level magus" ability="This functions like the ‹arcana/reflection› magus arcana, except it only works on spells that deal acid, cold, electricity, or fire damage. As long as the elemental knight spends at least 1 point from his *arcane pool* to activate this arcana, he may expend rounds of *elemental assault* as if they were *arcane pool* points. For example, the knight could spend 1 *arcane pool* point and 3 rounds of *elemental assault* to reflect a spell of 4th-level or lower."}

::ab[Energy Resistance Boost (Su)]{next icon=def swift="The elemental knight can expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* or 1 round of *elemental assault* to increase all of his racial energy resistances by +5 for 1 round."}

::ab[Elemental Matrix (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="An elemental knight gains ‹feat/Incremental Elemental Assault› as a bonus feat." order=aw swift="An elemental knight may expend rounds of duration from his *elemental assault* ability. For every 4 rounds expended, he gains 1 *arcane pool* point." replace="Spell recall"}
@HL[Magus Arcana:] The following magus arcana complement the elemental knight archetype: ‹arcana/empowered magic›, ‹arcana/pool strike›, ‹arcana/spell shield›.

esoteric.name: Esoteric
description: ## Esoteric
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/120›

Esoterics are drawn to the mysticism of the occult and spend their lives delving into forgotten texts and forbidden tomes. Each esoteric learns to inscribe his spells on his flesh in a complex series of occult symbols and arcane equations.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="An esoteric is proficient with simple weapons only." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down-magic ability="An esoteric has one fewer spell slot of each level than a regular magus. If this reduces his number of spell slots of a particular level to zero, he can cast spells of that level only if his Intelligence allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Arcane Pool (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee ability="An esoteric can use his *arcane pool* to enhance his unarmed strikes as if they were manufactured weapons." imp5="He can use these bonuses to add any of the following weapon special abilities to his weapons or unarmed strikes: ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/impact›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, ‹magic-enh/speed›, or ‹magic-enh/thundering›." alter="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{l=1 icon=power head ability="An esoteric gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. He also treats his magus levels as ‹class/monk› levels when determining the amount of damage he deals with his unarmed strikes."}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{next flavor="Relevant text from the monk class ability." icon=melee l1="Your unarmed strike deals 1d4 (Small), 1d6 (Medium), or 1d8 (Large) damage." imp4="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d6 (Small), 1d8 (Medium), or 2d6 (Large) damage." imp8="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d8 (Small), 1d10 (Medium), or 2d8 (Large) damage." imp12="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d10 (Small), 2d6 (Medium), or 3d6 (Large) damage." imp16="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d6 (Small), 2d8 (Medium), or 3d8 (Large) damage." imp20="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d8 (Small), 2d10 (Medium), or 4d8 (Large) damage."}

::ab[Unarmed Spellstrike (Su)]{l=1 icon=down ability="An esoteric gains the magus *spellstrike* class feature. However, an esoteric can deliver spells with *spellstrike* only with his unarmed strikes." alter=Spellstrike}

::ab[Ki Arcana (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="An esoteric's *arcane pool* is also considered a *ki pool* for the purposes of meeting feat and ability requirements. Points from an esoteric's *arcane pool* can be used for abilities that normally require ki. If an esoteric gains a *ki pool,* he can use points from his *arcane pool* and *ki pool* interchangeably (though not special points such as *drunken ki*)." replace="Spell recall"}

::ab[Tattooed Spell (Sp)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="An esoteric can permanently tattoo one spell from his magus spellbook onto his body. The spell that is tattooed must be of 2nd-level or lower and not have an expensive material component or a focus." imp11="He can tattoo a second spell following the restrictions above, except the spell must be of 4th level or lower." imp17="He can tattoo a third spell following the restrictions above, except the spell can be of any level." special="The esoteric can prepare any tattooed spell without needing his spellbook. Additionally, he can cast each of his tattooed spells as a spell-like ability once per day. When the esoteric uses these spell-like abilities, the tattoo associated with the spell-like ability glows as ‹spell/light› for a number of rounds equal to the spell's level." replace="Bonus feats"}

::ab[AC Bonus (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def passive="When an esoteric wears light or no armor, he gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC and CMD. He loses these bonuses while immobilized or helpless, or while wearing medium or heavy armor, using a shield, or carrying a medium or heavy load." imp13="The bonus increases to +2." replace="Medium armor~heavy armor"}
::ab[Improved Tattooed Spell (Sp)]{l=11 icon=boost ability="An esoteric can use each of his spell-like abilities from *tattooed spell* one additional time per day." replace="Improved spell recall"}

fiend_flayer.name: Fiend Flayer
description: ## Fiend Flayer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/170›

Some tiefling magi can tap the dark energy of their fiendish blood to enhance their arcane and combat talents. By physically carving away their tainted flesh, they can use its dark energies to enhance their powers, call forth weapons from thin air, and bypass enemies' strongest defenses. A fiend flayer has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Tiefling}

::ab[Infernal Mortification (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-down standard="A fiend flayer can sacrifice some of his own infernal blood to add to his *arcane pool.* For every 2 points of ‹rule/Constitution damage› the fiend flayer takes in this way, his *arcane pool* increases by 1 point. Any *arcane pool* points gained in this way and not spent disappear the next time the magus prepares his spells." special="Unlike normal ability score damage, this damage cannot be healed by way of ‹spell/lesser restoration›. Only time can heal the Constitution damage taken by way of *infernal mortification.* This ability cannot be used if the fiend flayer's Constitution damage is equal to or greater than 1/2 his Constitution score."}
::ab[Magus Arcana]{head icon=power ability="A fiend flayer gains access to the following magus arcana. He cannot select any arcana more than once."}

::ab[Fiendblade (Su)]{next icon=melee swift="As long as the fiend flayer used *infernal mortification* that day to increase his *arcane pool,* he can conjure forth a weapon using this arcana. Doing so costs 2 points from his *arcane pool.* The weapon can take the form of any single one-handed melee weapon the fiend flayer is proficient with. This weapon has a +1 enhancement bonus. This summoned weapon lasts for 1 minute." increment="The weapon's bonus~3~4" imp5="These bonuses can be used to add any of the following weapon properties to the *fiendblade:* ‹magic-enh/anarchic›, ‹magic-enh/axiomatic›, ‹magic-enh/dancing›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, ‹magic-enh/speed›, or ‹magic-enh/unholy›. These bonuses and properties are decided when the *arcane pool* points are spent and cannot be changed until the next time the fiend flayer uses this arcana." special="Another creature cannot wield the *fiendblade;* if it leaves the hand of the fiend flayer, it dissipates in a wisp of red smoke that smells of burning blood. A fiend flayer can only have one *fiendblade* in existence at a time. If he uses this ability again, the first *fiendblade* disappears."}

::ab[Bypassing Strike (Su)]{next icon=lower swift="The fiend flayer can expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to allow one melee or *spellstrike* attack he makes before the end of his turn to ignore an evil outsider target's damage reduction."}

greensting_slayer.name: Greensting Slayer
description: ## Greensting Slayer
‹SOURCE Bastards of Golarion/9›

The blending of martial prowess with elven magic has long been a staple of the Blackash Training Grounds in Erages. More than a few who study at Erages Academy follow this path because of the promises of wealth that the city's criminal element makes toward practiced brawlers and smugglers. Greensting slayers have the following class features.

::ab[Arcane Pool]{l=1 icon=power-down swift="A greensting slayer can expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to add 1d6 points of ‹ability/sneak attack› damage to his next melee attack that round. The attack must meet all of the usual prerequisites for making a sneak attack, as the ‹class/rogue› class feature." increment="The amount of sneak attack damage~1~4" incrementEnd="d6." special="If a greensting slayer gets a sneak attack from another source, the bonuses on damage stack. A greensting slayer cannot use his *arcane pool* to enhance his weapon." alter="Arcane pool"}
::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def passive="The greensting slayer gains ‹ability/evasion›, as the rogue class feature." replace="Medium armor"}
::ab[Improved Evasion (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="The greensting slayer gains ‹talent/improved evasion›, as the advanced rogue talent of the same name." replace="Heavy armor"}

::ab[Magus Arcana]{head icon=power info="In addition to the new magus arcana presented below, the following magus arcana complement the greensting slayer archetype: ‹arcana/close range›, ‹arcana/hasted assault›, ‹arcana/maneuver mastery›, ‹arcana/pool strike›, and ‹arcana/silent magic›."}

::ab[Dark Shifter (Su)]{next move="The magus can expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to change the target of an ongoing spell effect with the ‹spelldef/darkness› descriptor within 60 feet. The new target must be within the spell's range, using the magus's level as the effective caster level for the purpose of determining the maximum range the effect can be moved from the magus." special="If the spell effect the magus is moving originated from another creature, the magus must succeed at a caster level check to reposition the effect (DC = 11 + the effect's caster level). Failure wastes the magus's move action, but does not expend the point from his *arcane pool.*" prereq="6th-level magus" icon=magic}

hexbreaker.name: Hexbreaker
description: ## Hexbreaker
‹SOURCE Blood of the Coven/27›

Hexbreakers - sometimes called witch-hammers for their proclivity toward warhammers - have borne witness to the damage of unrestrained arcane power and hone their own arcane arts to battle such abuse. They train themselves to shatter the curses and dark transformations that can haunt others for a lifetime.

::ab[Repel Curse (Su)]{l=4 icon=protect-learn flavor="A hexbreaker has the chance to protect others from being cursed." immediate="When a spell with the curse descriptor or a curse-style effect (such as a witch's ‹hex/evil eye› or ‹hex/misfortune› hex or a ‹monster/sea hag«'s› evil eye) targets a creature within 30 feet of him, a hexbreaker can spend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to attempt to dispel that effect, as if he had cast ‹spell/remove curse›. This ability has no effect on ongoing effects, such as a sea hag's horrific appearance or a ‹monster/pugwampi«'s› unluck aura." order=ia ability="The magus adds *remove curse* to his spell list as a 3rd-level spell and adds ‹spell/break enchantment› as a 4th-level spell." replace="Spell recall"}

::ab[Counter Curse (Su)]{l=11 icon=protect ability="Whenever a hexbreaker successfully dispels a curse effect or spell with the curse descriptor using his *repel curse* ability, he can immediately spend 2 points from his *arcane pool* to reflect the spell back on the original caster. This otherwise functions as ‹spell/spell turning›." replace="Improved spell recall"}
::ab[Hexbreaker Arcana]{head icon=power info="A hexbreaker can select from the following arcana in addition to those normally available."}

::ab[Improved Remove Curse (Su)]{next icon=boost ability="The hexbreaker can spend additional points from his *arcane pool* to improve his ability to remove a curse. Each point spent while casting *remove curse* or *break enchantment* grants a +2 bonus to his caster level check. He can use this arcana after rolling the caster level check, but only before the results are revealed. This can be used with the hexbreaker's *repel curse* and *counter curse* abilities."}

::ab[Wracking Dispel (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="Whenever the hexbreaker successfully dispels a spell using *break enchantment,* ‹spell/dispel magic›, or *remove curse,* he can expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to wrack the original caster with pain, dealing 1d4 points of damage per spell level of the effect dispelled. If the target succeeds at a Will save (DC = 15 + the level of the spell dispelled + the hexbreaker's Intelligence modifier) it takes only 1 point of damage per spell level. The hexbreaker cannot target the same caster more than once per round with this ability, regardless of how many effects he dispels."}

**Magus Arcana:** The following magus arcana complement the hexbreaker: ‹arcana/bane blade›, ‹arcana/dispelling strike›, ‹arcana/disruptive›, ‹arcana/lingering pain›, ‹arcana/spellbreaker›, and ‹arcana/reflection›.

hexcrafter.name: Hexcrafter
description: ## Hexcrafter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/48›

A hexcrafter magus has uncovered the secret of using his *arcane pool* to recreate ‹class/witch› hexes. These magi can hex friend and foe, curse those they strike, and expand their spell selection to include many curses and harmful spells.

::ab[Hex Magus (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="The hexcrafter magus gains access to a small number of witch ‹ability/hexes›. The hexcrafter magus picks one hex from the witch's *hex* class feature. He gains the benefit of or uses that hex as if he were a witch of a level equal to his magus level." replace="Spell recall"}

::ab[Hex Arcana]{head icon=power ability="A hexcrafter gains access to the following magus arcana, or may select any witch hex in place of a magus arcana." imp12="The hexcrafter may select a hex or ‹ability/major hex<es› in place of a magus arcana." imp18="A hexcrafter can select a hex, major hex, or ‹ability/grand hex<es› in place of a magus arcana." special="He cannot select any hex or arcana more than once."}

::ab[Accursed Strike (Sp)]{next icon=magic ability="A hexcrafter magus who can cast ‹spell/bestow curse›, ‹spell/major curse›, or any spell with the ‹spelldef/curse› descriptor can deliver these prepared spells using the *spellstrike* ability, even if the spells are not touch attack spells."}

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn info="A hexcrafter magus adds the following spells to his magus spell list: *bestow curse, major curse,* and all other spells of 6th level or lower that have the curse descriptor."}
**Magus Arcana:** The following magus arcana complement the hexcrafter archetype: ‹arcana/arcane accuracy›, ‹arcana/broad study›, ‹arcana/familiar›, and ‹arcana/quickened magic›.

iron_ring_striker.name: Iron-Ring Striker
description: ## Iron-Ring Striker
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/5›
Practitioners of this martial art, powered by arcane magic, wear an iron ring on the finger of one hand. This legacy has been handed down from the tradition's Vudrani founders.
::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=down info="An iron-ring striker is not proficient with martial weapons." alter="Weapon proficiency"}

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down ability="An iron-ring striker has one fewer spell slot of each level than a regular magus. If this reduces his number of spell slots of a particular level to 0, he can cast spells of that level only if his Intelligence allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Arcane Pool (Su)]{icon=melee ability="An iron-ring striker can use his *arcane pool* to enhance his unarmed strikes as if they were manufactured weapons." imp5="He can use these bonuses to add any of the following weapon special abilities to his weapons or unarmed strikes: ‹magic-enh/advancing›, ‹magic-enh/cruel›, ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/guardian›, ‹magic-enh/impact›, ‹magic-enh/invigorating›, ‹magic-enh/mimetic›, ‹magic-enh/negating›, ‹magic-enh/speed›, and ‹magic-enh/vicious›." alter="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Unarmed Spellstrike (Su)]{icon=down ability="An iron-ring striker gains the magus *spellstrike* class feature. However, an iron-ring striker can deliver spells with *spellstrike* with only unarmed strikes." alter=Spellstrike}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{icon=power-melee head ability="An iron-ring striker gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. He deals unarmed strike damage as per a ‹class/monk› of his level. For the purpose of determining damage, these levels stack with levels from any other class that grants increased damage to unarmed strikes."}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{next flavor="Relevant text from the monk class ability." icon=melee l1="Your unarmed strike deals 1d4 (Small), 1d6 (Medium), or 1d8 (Large) damage." imp4="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d6 (Small), 1d8 (Medium), or 2d6 (Large) damage." imp8="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d8 (Small), 1d10 (Medium), or 2d8 (Large) damage." imp12="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d10 (Small), 2d6 (Medium), or 3d6 (Large) damage." imp16="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d6 (Small), 2d8 (Medium), or 3d8 (Large) damage." imp20="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d8 (Small), 2d10 (Medium), or 4d8 (Large) damage."}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=5 icon=down ability="An iron-ring striker must select his bonus feat from this list: ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush›, ‹feat/Improved Disarm›, ‹feat/Improved Sunder›, or ‹feat/Improved Trip›. He doesn't need to meet the prerequisites normally required for these feats." alter="5th-level bonus feat"}

::ab[Empower Combat (Su)]{l=7 icon=boost flavor="An iron-ring striker learns to use magic to grant himself the power of a larger creature." standard="He can expend one non-cantrip prepared magus spell to treat his size as if it were larger for the purposes of determining the ‹rule/combat_maneuvers>«size modifier to his CMB and CMD›, as well as which creatures he can affect with combat maneuvers. For every level of the spell he sacrifices, his effective size increases by one step, up to a maximum of Colossal. For every two effective size increments he increases, he gains a +2 size bonus to Strength, to a maximum of +6. This does not change the iron-ring striker's actual size. This effect lasts for 1 minute per level of the spell sacrificed." replace="Medium armor~heavy armor"}

::ab[Spell Maneuvers (Su)]{l=7 icon=melee ability="As long as an iron-ring striker is wearing light armor or no armor, he can use his *unarmed spellstrike* ability as part of a combat maneuver check to ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/sunder›, or ‹rule/trip› an opponent." replace="Knowledge pool"}

::ab[Reflexive Spell Maneuver (Ex)]{l=16 icon=def-magic immediate="When an iron-ring striker is the target of a combat maneuver check to bull rush, disarm, sunder, or trip, he can target his attacker with a magus spell he has prepared with a range of touch. If the spell would normally require a touch attack, an iron-ring striker can attempt a combat maneuver check for this attack instead." special="The iron-ring striker must be wearing light armor or no armor to use reflexive spell maneuver." replace=Counterstrike}

jistkan_artificer.name: Jistkan Artificer
description: ## Jistkan Artificer
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/17›
As part of studying the secret methods the original Jistkan artificers used to build golems, these magi graft construct parts onto their own arms.

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down info="A Jistkan artificer has one fewer spell slot of each level than a regular magus. If this reduces his number of spell slots of a particular level to zero, he can cast spells of that level only if his Intelligence allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Golem Arm (Ex)]{icon=melee-boost head ability="A Jistkan artificer begins play with a *golem arm* grafted in place of one of his arms. The *golem arm* is a ‹rule/masterwork w«eapon› that is treated as an unarmed strike and deals damage as if he were a ‹class/monk› 2 levels lower than his class level (minimum 1st level). The *golem arm* is made from basic parts and mundane materials that grant no additional benefits. A Jistkan artificer can recreate his *golem arm* from other materials by spending 1 day and paying an additional +50% of the normal cost of the special material used in the creation of this new *golem arm.* For the purposes of determining the weapon type and weight for special materials, the *golem arm* is treated as a ‹eq-weapon/heavy mace›." special="If a Jistkan artificer recreates his *golem arm,* any previously used special materials are destroyed. The arm gains no benefits from items that enhance unarmed strikes, such as an ‹magic-wondrous/amulet of mighty fists›." imp1="A Jistkan artificer can use his *arcane pool* to enhance his unarmed strikes as if they were manufactured weapons." imp5="He can use this ability to add any of the following special abilities to his unarmed strikes: ‹magic-enh/corrosive›, ‹magic-enh/corrosive burst›, ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/impact›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, or ‹magic-enh/thundering›." imp11="He he gains access to the following special abilities: ‹magic-enh/brilliant energy› and ‹magic-enh/speed›." alter="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{next flavor="Relevant text from the monk class ability." icon=melee l1="Your unarmed strike deals 1d4 (Small), 1d6 (Medium), or 1d8 (Large) damage." imp4="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d6 (Small), 1d8 (Medium), or 2d6 (Large) damage." imp8="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d8 (Small), 1d10 (Medium), or 2d8 (Large) damage." imp12="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d10 (Small), 2d6 (Medium), or 3d6 (Large) damage." imp16="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d6 (Small), 2d8 (Medium), or 3d8 (Large) damage."}

::ab[Improved Unarmed Strike]{icon=power-down ability="A Jistkan artificer gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat." special="He prepares one fewer cantrip per day than normal." alter=Cantrips}

::ab[Empowered Arm (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A Jistkan artificer's *golem arm* is treated as a magic weapon and gains a +1 enhancement bonus on attack and damage rolls." increment="This bonus~3~4" replace="The magus arcana gained at 3rd level"}
::ab[Unarmed Spellstrike (Su)]{icon=down info="A Jistkan artificer can use *spellstrike* to deliver spells only when attacking with his unarmed strikes." alter=Spellstrike}

::ab[Magus Arcana]{head icon=power ability="A Jistkan artificer gains access to the following arcana in addition to those normally available to the magus. He cannot select any arcana more than once."}

::ab[Break Spell (Ex)]{next prereq="13th-level magus and the *shielding arm* magus arcana." icon=def ability="A Jistkan artificer can use his arm to disrupt spells targeted at him. When a ranged attack generated by a spell effect targets the Jistkan artificer, he can spend a point from his *arcane pool* to attempt a melee attack roll as an attack of opportunity. If the result is greater than the total attack roll of the ranged attack, the attack is negated. Spell effects that do not require attack rolls cannot be deflected."}

::ab[Flurrying Arm (Ex)]{next prereq="11th-level magus" icon=power-melee ability="A Jistkan artificer can improve his *golem arm* to attack faster. He gains the flurry of blows monk class ability, treating his class level as his monk level. A Jistkan artificer makes only one additional attack when he uses flurry of blows."}

::ab[Sharpened Arm (Ex)]{next icon=boost ability="A Jistkan artificer can rework his arm to more closely resemble a blade. He can choose to deal piercing or slashing damage with his *golem arm.* When he does so, the *golem arm* threatens a critical hit on 19-20. The artificer also adds ‹magic-enh/keen› and ‹magic-enh/wounding› to his list of *arcane pool* weapon abilities."}

::ab[Shielding Arm (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="A Jistkan artificer learns to deflect blows with his arm. If he is not wielding any other weapons, he gains a shield bonus to his AC equal to his *golem arm's* enhancement bonus."}

kapenia_dancer.name: Kapenia Dancer
description: ## Kapenia Dancer
‹SOURCE Varisia, Birthplace of Legends/10›

For some Varisians, their traditional dances pulse with the power of their ancestors. These dancers incorporate their kapenias - scarves embroidered with the history of the bearer's clan - into their dances, weaving deadly whirling steps with the power of their ancestors.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down info="A kapenia dancer is proficient in simple weapons and ‹eq-weapon/bladed scarves›. Additionally, a kapenia dancer can wield a bladed scarf as a one-handed melee weapon. A kapenia dancer is not proficient with armor or shields and suffers the normal arcane spell failure chance when casting magus spells while armored."}

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down info="A kapenia dancer can cast one fewer spell of each level than normal. If this reduces the number to 0, he can cast spells of that level only if his Intelligence allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level."}

::ab[Canny Defense (Ex)]{l=1 head icon=def ability="When a kapenia dancer is wielding a bladed scarf, he gains the *canny defense* ability. This is identical to the ‹pclass/duelist« prestige class› ability of the same name, save that his magus level (rather than his duelist level) affects his Armor Class."}

::ab[From the Duelist class]{next icon=def passive="When wearing light or no armor and not using a shield, a duelist adds 1 point of Intelligence bonus (if any) per duelist class level as a dodge bonus to her Armor Class while wielding a melee weapon. If a duelist is caught ‹rule/flat-footed› or otherwise denied her Dexterity bonus, she also loses this bonus."}
::ab[Weapon Focus (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A kapenia dancer gains ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (bladed scarf) as a bonus feat."}
::ab[Kapenia Dancer Arcana]{head icon=power ability="A kapenia dancer gains access to the following magus arcana. He cannot select any arcana more than once."}

::ab[Elasticity (Su)]{next icon=boost free="The kapenia dancer can spend 1 point from his *arcane pool* in order to grant a bladed scarf he's wielding reach until the end of his turn. A kapenia dancer can use this arcana in tandem with other magus arcana."}

::ab[Kapenia Curse (Su)]{next icon=lower order=ca choice="Choose an ability score upon taking this arcana." ability="Whenever the kapenia dancer scores a critical hit with a bladed scarf, he may expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to deal 1d4 points of ‹rule/ability_>damage› to that ability score of the creature struck." special="This arcana can be selected multiple times, but a different ability score must be chosen each time." prereq="9th-level magus"}

::ab[Spirit Fingers (Su)]{next icon=melee swift="Whenever the kapenia dancer scores a hit with a melee weapon, he can spend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to immediately attempt a ‹rule/steal› combat maneuver."}

::ab[Spirit Step (Su)]{next prereq="12th-level magus" icon=magic ability="Whenever the kapenia dancer scores a critical hit with a bladed scarf, he may expend 1 point from his *arcane pool* to teleport to any point within 30 feet. The kapenia dancer's turn ends after being teleported."}

**Magus Arcana:** The following magus arcana complement the kapenia dancer archetype: ‹arcana/arcane cloak›, ‹arcana/dispelling strike›, ‹arcana/hasted assault›, ‹arcana/maneuver mastery›, ‹arcana/prescient attack›, ‹arcana/prescient defense›.

kensai.name: Kensai
description: ## Kensai
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/55;Adventurer's Armory 2/11›

A kensai spends his life focusing his training and meditation into a rapturous perfection of the use of a single weapon, which is usually but not always a sword, channeling his arcane might through it in a dizzying and deadly dance beyond the abilities of even the greatest of mundane warriors.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down info="A kensai is proficient in simple weapons and in a single martial or exotic melee weapon of his choice. A kensai is not proficient with armor or shields and suffers normal arcane spell failure chance when casting magus spells while armored."}

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down info="A kensai may cast one fewer spell of each level than normal. If this reduces the number to 0, he may cast spells of that level only if his Intelligence allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level."}

::ab[Canny Defense (Ex)]{l=1 head icon=def ability="When a kensai is wielding his chosen weapon, he gains the *canny defense* ability. This is identical to the ‹pclass/duelist« prestige class› ability of the same name, save that his chosen weapon may be of any type"}

::ab[From the Duelist class]{next icon=def passive="When wearing light or no armor and not using a shield, a duelist adds 1 point of Intelligence bonus (if any) per duelist class level as a dodge bonus to her Armor Class while wielding a melee weapon. If a duelist is caught ‹rule/flat-footed› or otherwise denied her Dexterity bonus, she also loses this bonus."}
::ab[Weapon Focus (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A kensai gains either ‹feat/Weapon Focus› or ‹feat/Modified Weapon Proficiency› with his chosen weapon as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Perfect Strike (Ex)]{l=4 icon=melee-boost ability="When a kensai hits with his chosen weapon, he can spend 1 point from his *arcane pool* in order to maximize his weapon damage. Don't roll for damage: the weapon deals maximum damage. This affects only the weapon's base damage dice, not additional damage from ‹ability/sneak attack›, magical weapon properties, *spellstrike,* or critical hits." special="If the kensai confirms a critical hit, he can instead spend 2 points from his *arcane pool* to increase his weapon's critical multiplier by 1." replace="Spell recall"}

::ab[Fighter Training (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power passive="A kensai counts his magus level - 3 as his fighter level for the purpose of qualifying for feats (if he has levels in fighter, these levels stack), but forfeits the benefit of such feats with weapons other than his favored weapon." replace="Knowledge pool"}

::ab[Iaijutsu (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost passive="A kensai applies his Intelligence modifier as well as his Dexterity modifier on initiative rolls (minimum 0)." ability="A kensai may make attacks of opportunity when flat-footed, and may draw his favored weapon as a @HLfree_action as part of taking an attack of opportunity." replace="Medium armor" order=pa}

::ab[Critical Perfection (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost-power ability="A kensai adds his Intelligence bonus (minimum 0) on critical hit confirmation rolls with his favored weapon. In addition, the kensai may use his magus levels in place of his base attack bonuses to qualify for ‹feat/Critical Focus› and any feat for which it is a prerequisite; these feats apply only with a kensai's favored weapon." replace="Magus arcana gained at 9th level"}

::ab[Superior Reflexes (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="Kensai can make a number of attacks of opportunity in a round equal to his Intelligence modifier (minimum 1). This effect stacks with the ‹feat/Combat Reflexes› feat." replace="Improved spell recall"}

::ab[Iaijutsu Focus (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power-boost ability="A kensai may always act and may draw his weapon as a @HLswift_action during a surprise round, though he is considered flat-footed until he acts. During a surprise round or when attacking a flat-footed opponent, he adds his Intelligence modifier on damage with his chosen weapon (minimum 0)." replace="Heavy armor"}
::ab[Iaijutsu Master (Ex)]{l=19 icon=boost-roll ability="A kensai's initiative roll is automatically a natural 20 and he is never surprised." replace="Greater spell access"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="A kensai gains *weapon mastery* with his favored weapon, as the ‹class/fighter› class ability." replace="True magus"}

**Magus Arcana:** The following magus arcana complement the kensai archetype: ‹arcana/concentrate›, ‹arcana/critical strike›, ‹arcana/prescient attack›, ‹arcana/prescient defense›, ‹arcana/spell shield›.

magic_warrior.name: Magic Warrior
description: ## Magic Warrior
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/123;Inner Sea Intrigue/42›

Trained in a tradition stretching back to Old-Mage Jatembe's Ten Magic Warriors, Magic Warriors renounce their identities to master magical might and serve as champions of culture and learning across the Mwangi Expanse and beyond. The original Ten Magic Warriors each directly served Old-Mage Jatembe as guardians, bodyguards, allies, and friends. Tales of their deeds are still told today in the ‹rule/Magaambya›, and while they are long gone, the traditions that rose from these tales live on.

While the original Ten Magic Warriors championed unusual skills drawn from various arcane spellcasting classes, often combined with martial classes or even prestige classes, the tradition today has been standardized over the course of the intervening centuries into an archetype followed strictly by magi. Even then, it's not uncommon for a Magic Warrior to multiclass with a martial class for a few levels or to become an ‹pclass/eldritch knight› to further bolster her combat powers. No one path is regarded as canonical for the magi of the Magic Warriors, and they value their diversity, looking to their brothers and sisters constantly for inspiration regarding new ways to defend the Magaambya and the people of the Mwangi Expanse from the multitude of dangers the world has to offer.
::cskill{gain=kh~kna lose=kd~kp}

::ab[Nameless Mask (Ex)]{icon=def-down-warning ability="A Magic Warrior gains strength by hiding her identity behind a mask that represents an animal, but also suffers drawbacks from doing so. While wearing her mask, a Magic Warrior gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against divinations in the scrying subschool. However, NPCs who are unfamiliar with or have a poor opinion of the Magic Warrior tradition have a starting attitude of unfriendly toward the Magic Warrior while she wears her mask. If using the ‹rule/influence« system›, she requires one additional success to increase her influence level over such an NPC." special="The Magic Warrior cannot ever reveal her name or remove her mask in front of anyone who she has never met before. If she violates this important taboo, she must discard her mask and create or obtain a new mask and wear it before she can gain any benefits that result from wearing her mask."}

::ab[Magic Warrior's Aspect (Su)]{l=3 icon=power standard="A Magic Warrior can gain an aspect of an animal's might by expending 1 point from her *arcane pool.* The animal must be the same one that the Magic Warrior's mask represents (the animal can change if the Magic Warrior creates or obtains a new mask). The Magic Warrior gains one ability the animal has from the list of abilities given in ‹spell/beast shape I› (this ability doesn't allow her to change her size). The ability lasts for 1 minute per class level." replace="Magus arcana gained at 3rd level"}

::ab[Nameless Anonymity (Su)]{l=8 icon=def-down flavor="A Magic Warrior's masked identity further protects her from divinations." ability="Once per day, as long as she is in her masked identity, she can cast ‹spell/nondetection› on herself, using her masked warrior level as her caster level." special="A Magic Warrior gains *improved spell combat* at 14th level instead of at 8th level." alter="Improved spell combat" replace="Greater spell combat"}

::ab[Magaambya Spell Access (Su)]{l=19 icon=learn ability="A Magic Warrior learns ancient secrets to using natural magic. She learns seven spells from the ‹main/spells_druid«'s spell list› and places them in her spellbook as magus spells of the same level at which they appear on the druid spell list. She gains one druid spell not on the magus spell list for each of the listed spell levels: 0 level, 1st level, 2nd level, 3rd level, 4th level, 5th level, and 6th level. She can ignore the divine focus component of these spells." replace="Greater spell access"}

**Magus Arcana:** The following magus arcana complement the Magic Warrior archetype: ‹arcana/accurate strike›, ‹arcana/arcane accuracy›, ‹arcana/arcane cloak›, ‹arcana/arcane edge›, ‹arcana/bane blade›, ‹arcana/devoted blade›, ‹arcana/empowered magic›, ‹arcana/maximized magic›, ‹arcana/quickened magic›, ‹arcana/silent magic›, ‹arcana/spell blending›, and ‹arcana/still magic›.

mindblade.name: Mindblade
description: ## Mindblade
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/121›
A mindblade blends psychic talent and martial skill to lethal effect. By forming weapons with her mind, she always has the right tool for any situation.
:::ab{icon=magic action=Ability title=Spells replace=Spellcasting}

A mindblade casts spells from the magus spell list as psychic spells. She can cast any spell she knows without preparing it ahead of time. To learn or cast a spell, a mindblade must have an Intelligence score equal to at least 10 + the spell's level. The saving throw DC to resist a mindblade's spell is equal to 10 + the spell's level + the mindblade's Intelligence modifier.

Like other spellcasters, a mindblade can cast only a certain number of spells of each spell level per day. She knows the same number of spells and receives the same number of spells slots per day as a ‹class/bard› of her magus level, and knows and uses 0-level knacks as a bard uses cantrips. In addition, she receives ‹rule/bonus spells« per day› if she has a high Intelligence score.

At 5th level and every 3 levels thereafter, a mindblade can learn a new spell in place of one she already knows, using the same rules as a bard. In effect, the mindblade loses the old spell in exchange for the new one. The new spell's level must be the same as that of the spell being exchanged, and the new spell must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level spell the mindblade can cast.
A mindblade need not prepare her spells in advance. She can cast any magus spell she knows at any time, assuming she has not yet used up her allotment of spells per day for the spell's level.

::ab[Psychic Pool (Su)]{icon=magic-melee ability="A mindblade gains a *psychic pool,* similar to a normal magus's *arcane pool.*" imp1="A mindblade can expend 1 point from her *psychic pool* as a @HLstandard_action to manifest a light melee weapon of her choice, formed from psychic energy. By spending 2 points, the mindblade can manifest a one-handed melee weapon, and by spending 3 points, she can manifest a two-handed melee weapon (but not a ‹misc/double› weapon). This psychic weapon can last indefinitely, but it vanishes if it leaves the mindblade's hand. The mindblade can dismiss a held psychic weapon as a @HLfree_action. When a psychic weapon vanishes, the mindblade regains the psychic energy used to create it. She can maintain only one weapon at a time. A psychic weapon counts as a magic weapon of whatever type the mindblade selected, with an enhancement bonus equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-third› of her magus level, to a maximum of +5 at 12th level. At 15th and 18th levels, the weapon gains an additional +1 enhancement bonus, which the mindblade can spend only on weapon special abilities (see below)." imp5="The mindblade can add any of the weapon special abilities listed in the *arcane pool* class feature in place of these bonuses, although the weapon must maintain at least a +1 bonus to benefit from any weapon special abilities." special="This ability counts as *arcane pool* for the purpose of feats, abilities, and class features." replace="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Psychic Access (Su)]{l=4 icon=learn ability="The mindblade gains access to an expanded spell list. She adds two spells from the ‹main/spells_>psychic« class spell list› to her magus spell list as magus spells of the same spell level. These must be spells of levels the mindblade is able to cast." repeatAt="She adds two more psychic class spells to her spell list, following the same restrictions.~7~11~14~19" special="When a mindblade takes the ‹arcana/spell blending› arcana, she gains spells from the psychic class spell list instead of the wizard spell list." replace="Spell recall~knowledge pool~improved spell recall~greater spell combat~greater spell access"}

::ab[Dual Weapons (Su)]{icon=power-melee l7="A mindblade can maintain two psychic weapons at a time or a psychic double weapon, though each weapon (or each end of a double weapon) has an enhancement bonus 1 lower than normal." l12="Each of the two psychic weapons (or each end of a double weapon) instead has an enhancement bonus 2 lower than normal. When using two-weapon fighting with two psychic weapons or a psychic double weapon, the mindblade can use her *spell combat* ability as though she had a hand free." replace="Medium armor"}
::ab[Rapid Manifest (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost swift="A mindblade can manifest a psychic weapon as a swift action." replace="Improved spell combat"}

::ab[Dual Manifest (Su)]{l=13 icon=boost ability="A mindblade can manifest two psychic weapons with the same action. She must pay the cost for both weapons. Also, when wielding a weapon two-handed, she can use her *spell combat* ability as though she had a hand free." replace="Heavy armor"}

myrmidarch.name: Myrmidarch
description: ## Myrmidarch
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/56›

The myrmidarch is a skilled specialist, using magic to supplement and augment his martial mastery. Less inclined to mix the two than a typical magus, the myrmidarch seeks supremacy with blade, bow, and armor.

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down ability="Myrmidarchs cast one fewer spell of each level than normal. If this reduces the number to 0, he may cast spells of that level only if his Intelligence allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level."}

::ab[Ranged Spellstrike (Su)]{l=4 icon=ranged ability="A myrmidarch can use *spellstrike* to cast a single-target touch attack ranged spell and deliver it through a ranged weapon attack. Even if the spell can normally affect multiple targets, only a single missile, ray, or effect accompanies the attack." imp11="A myrmidarch can spend a @HLfull-round_action to cast a spell that includes multiple ranged touch attacks and deliver more than one using ranged attacks. The spell must have a casting time of 1 full-round action or less. This functions similarly to the base ability of *ranged spellstrike,* but the myrmidarch can deliver a number of missiles, rays, or effect up to the number of attacks he could make with a full attack. Attacks beyond the first take penalties as if the myrmidarch were making a full attack. Any effects not used in the round the spell is cast are lost, as are any additional ranged touch attacks the spell would allow beyond the myrmidarch's maximum number of attacks." replace="Spell recall~improved spell recall"}

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=6 head icon=power-boost ability="A myrmidarch gains *weapon training,* as the ‹class/fighter› ability, adding an additional weapon group every six levels after 6th (to a maximum of three groups at 18th level) and increasing the bonus on attack and damage rolls for weapon groups already chosen by +1." replace="Magus arcana gained at 6th, 12th, and 18th levels"}

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{flavor="Text from the fighter class ability, with levels adjusted." next icon=boost l6="Select one ‹ability/weapon group<s›. Whenever you attack with a weapon from this group, you gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls. You also add this bonus to any combat maneuver checks made with weapons from this group. This bonus also applies to your ‹rule/CMD› when defending against ‹rule/disarm› and ‹rule/sunder› attempts made against weapons from this group." l12="You select a new weapon group and gain the +1 bonus when using weapons from that group. Your previously selected group's bonus increases to +2." l18="You select a new weapon group and gain the +1 bonus when using weapons from that group. Your previously selected groups' bonuses increase to +2 and +3." special="Bonuses granted from overlapping groups do not stack. Take the highest bonus granted for a weapon if it resides in two or more groups."}

::ab[Fighter Training (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power-boost passive="A myrmidarch counts his magus level - 3 as his fighter level for the purpose of qualifying for feats (if he has levels in fighter, these levels stack)." imp10="The myrmidarch treats his magus levels as fighter levels for the purposes of *fighter training.*" replace="Knowledge pool~10th-level fighter training"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def head ability="A myrmidarch gains *armor training,* as the fighter ability, but he only receives the first two benefits, and at a slower rate (see below)." replace="Improved spell combat~greater spell combat"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{icon=boost next flavor="Text from the fighter class ability, with levels adjusted." l8="Whenever you wear armor, you reduce the armor check penalty by 1 (to a minimum of 0) and increase the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by the armor by 1. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing medium armor." l14="The penalty is now reduced by 2 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +2. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing heavy armor."}
::ab[Armor Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="A myrmidarch gains DR 5/- when wearing armor." replace="True magus"}

**Magus Arcana:** The following magus arcana complement the myrmidarch archetype: ‹arcana/accurate strike›, ‹arcana/arcane edge›, ‹arcana/lingering pain›, ‹arcana/prescient attack›, ‹arcana/prescient defense›, ‹arcana/spell shield›, ‹arcana/spellbreaker›.

nature_bonded_magus.name: Nature-Bonded Magus
description: ## Nature-Bonded Magus
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/92›
A nature-bonded magus synergizes arcane magic and the divine magic traditions of druids into a deadly synthesis.

::ab[Natural Magic]{icon=learn ability="A nature-bonded magus adds one 0-level and one 1st-level spell from the ‹main/druid spell« list› to the magus spell list and his spellbook. Each time a nature-bonded magus gains the ability to cast a new level of spells, he can add one spell of that level from the druid spell list to both the magus spell list and his spellbook."}

::ab[Plant Familiar]{icon=power ability="A nature-bonded magus gains the ‹arcana/familiar› magus arcana. A nature-bonded magus must choose a plant familiar as his familiar." special="Plant ‹ability/familiars› include the creeper ivy, dweomer cap, flowering lattice, petrifern, ravenous tumbleweed, razor fern, and suture vine." replace="Arcane pool"}

::ab[Familiar Symbiosis (Su)]{l=4 icon=power-def standard="When a nature-bonded magus is adjacent to his familiar, he can merge with his familiar; both the nature-bonded magus and the familiar provoke attacks of opportunity with this action. Doing so causes the plant familiar to bond with a part of the nature-bonded magus's body as though it were growing on him. While merged, the nature-bonded magus's familiar can take no actions (but can still attempt Perception checks and communicate with the nature-bonded magus), and it can't be targeted or harmed by effects." special="As long as his familiar is merged, the nature-bonded magus gains half his plant familiar's natural armor adjustment to AC. The nature-bonded magus can separate from his plant familiar as a @HLstandard_action that provokes attacks of opportunity for both of them. Once they have separated, the familiar appears in an adjacent square of the nature-bonded magus' choosing." replace="Spell recall"}

::ab[Woodland Stride (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A nature-bonded magus can move through any sort of undergrowth (such as natural briars, overgrown areas, thorns, and similar terrain) at his normal speed and without taking damage or suffering any other impairment. Briars, overgrown areas, and thorns that are enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion, however, still affect him." replace="Knowledge pool"}

::ab[Improved Familiar Symbiosis (Su)]{l=11 icon=boost passive="When a nature-bonded magus is merged with his familiar, he gains a +4 enhancement bonus to his Strength and Constitution." replace="Improved spell recall"}

puppetmaster.name: Puppetmaster
description: ## Puppetmaster
‹SOURCE Arcane Anthology/27›
Puppetmasters focus on using charm and illusion spells to control the senses of those for whom they perform.
::cskill{gain=kh~kl~kr~perf lose=climb~ride~swim}
::ab[Skills]{icon=info info="A puppetmaster gains 4 skill points per level." alter=Skills}

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn ability="A puppetmaster can cast spells from the bard and magus spell lists, and treats ‹main/bard spell«s› as magus spells. Because he does not have a bardic performance ability, some bard spells (which modify or require bardic performance) are poor choices for a puppetmaster. He must add bard spells to his spellbook prior to preparing them as normal. A puppetmaster can learn bard spells from a formula book or spellbook (if they are also alchemist extracts or wizard spells) or a scroll (as if learning a wizard spell from a scroll), or can select them to be one or more of the two magus spells he adds to his spellbook at each magus level." replace="Knowledge pool~greater spell access"}
